concept: Sibling / One of a shared origin
description: A person who shares a common origin, viewed as a parallel relationship of shared experience and mutual support. The role is defined by shared roots rather than hierarchy or age.
rationale: The word 'hora' is designed to feel balanced and parallel. The initial syllable 'ho-' uses the harmonious vowel 'o' to represent the shared origin or circle (Art Nouveau). The second syllable '-ra' uses the flowing 'r' and open 'a' to suggest a continuous, parallel relationship of mutual presence (Peace Linguistics, Buddhist).
